column7: Improves salinity tolerance by boosting plant vigor; careful N rate management needed in saline soils.
Helps overcome early saline stress through balanced nutrition; phosphorus improves ion uptake selectivity.
Acidifying effect beneficial in alkaline saline soils; sulfur may partially offset sodium effects.
Counteracts sodium uptake in saline soils; K+ competes with Na+ to improve ion selectivity.
Micronutrients (Fe, Zn, Cu) improve plant resilience in saline soils; brassinosteroid reduces Na-induced oxidative stress.
Humic acid chelates Na ions reducing plant uptake; nano-K improves K:Na selectivity in saline conditions.
Organic acids help leach excess salts from root zone; promotes salt-tolerant microbial communities in saline soils.
Azolla tolerant forms can grow in mildly saline water; organic N and organic matter help buffer soil salinity effects.
Foliar NPK bypasses saline soil nutrient barriers; direct leaf absorption improves nutrition despite salt-impaired root function.
